1 /

A digital experience to match the arena.

Website and mobile app. Pardubice 2030.

Shared at

15 June 2026

Created by

Alec Norton

MHAP

The commercial reality

In a modern arena, the ticket is no longer the business.

The revenue model

The promise of the arena only works if the fan flows.

In-venue spend now outweighs ticket sales. Cross-sell, upsell, food, merchandise, hospitality. Every euro beyond the ticket depends on one thing: the fan moving without friction from one product to the next. That flow is the revenue model.

image

The risk

Build it as five products and the fan feels five companies.

The biggest commercial risk on this programme

Fragmentation does not look like a failure. It looks like revenue you never see.

Every broken transition is a cross-sell that does not happen. The fan who buys a ticket but starts over to order food leaves the basket smaller. The reward that has no route back to a purchase never triggers the return visit. Nothing breaks and nothing shows up as a fault. The platform simply captures less of the spend the arena was built to earn, at every event, forever. That is the real return on getting this right, and the real cost of getting it wrong.

image

Our approach

We design the connections as the product.

Before a screen is drawn, we map the whole fan journey across every surface. Ticket to turnstile, merchandise to loyalty, app to arena. The connections between touchpoints are the deliverable, not an afterthought. This is service design, and it is the discipline that stops a platform fragmenting.

image

The system-level test

Every product earns its place. The system earns the fan's return.

We run every product through the same four questions, on its own and as part of the whole. The system-level pass is the one that kills fragmentation. We call it the LOVE framework.

L

Learn

Can a first-time fan understand what is on and what it costs within seconds?

O

Onboard

Is the first purchase effortless, and does it pull the fan toward the next one?

V

Value

Does the platform deliver recurring value before, during, and after every event?

E

Endorse

Does the experience earn the fan coming back, sharing, and bringing others?

Proof the connected system pays

allO: one system thought through as a whole, and the cross-sell follows.

We built allO across every product in the platform, not a single screen, designing the customer's continuous journey across the brand. When the system connects, the fan moves between products and buys more of them. Cross-sell and upsell stop being a marketing exercise bolted on top and become an outcome of the design. This is the return fragmentation leaves on the table, captured.

00%

Increase in average order value

0.0x

Items per transaction

00%

Repeat order rate

image

Proof it survives

FC Porto: the same commercial engine, proven at one of Europe's most demanding venues.

The features you are scoping are live there today. Secondary market, member wallet, on-behalf purchasing, in-stadium experience, Apple Pay and Google Pay. Built and run by us as the club's single ticketing channel, holding through matchday on-sale peaks.

17.1M

Page views, first full season

5.2M

Sessions, first full season

0.18%

Checkout error rate at peak

image

The long game

You will own this platform into the 2030s. Quality is what keeps it an asset.

We are likely the most expensive name on your shortlist. Here is what the difference buys.

A platform that runs for a decade is one of two things. An asset you can evolve cheaply as the arena grows, or a liability you are stuck maintaining. Which one it becomes is decided now, in the architecture and the code, not in 2032 when it is too late to change. We write simple, scalable software on purpose, because simple is what stays maintainable and legible to the engineers who inherit it. We staff this with senior practitioners across the full programme and no junior roles, because experience is what avoids the velocity-driven shortcuts a cheaper team takes under pressure. The premium is not a markup on the same product. It is the difference between a platform that stays an asset and one that does not.

Quality and speed are one decision. Under deadline pressure, the scope moves or the schedule moves. The quality bar does not.

How we deliver

One team, both surfaces, a decision point at every gate.

You are never locked in. Each phase produces an agreed artefact, and the next phase only starts when you accept it.

A single joint Discovery across both tenders. We map the full fan journey across both surfaces and run technical discovery against the highest-risk integrations first. The output is a set of clickable prototypes, built in code, that become the source of truth for everything that follows.

Deliverables

Validated clickable prototypes

Built in code. The single source of truth.

Assumptions register

Every unknown carried at stated risk, not hidden in the price.

Prioritised backlog and release plan

Agreed with you before we leave Discovery.

Build-ready design systems, one for web and one for app, each serving both brands through configurable branding. Accessibility built into every component. A rebrand becomes a configuration change, not a redesign.

Deliverables

Web and app design systems

Consistent, configurable for both brands.

Validated critical journeys

Every key path tested against the system-level standard before build.

The core build. 24 months of parallel website and app delivery on a common foundation, accepted incrementally. Each release is built, reviewed, tested, and accepted against criteria agreed up front, so risk stays in small validated increments rather than one big-bang acceptance at the end.

Deliverables

Progressive accepted releases

Each one done before the next begins.

Release-candidate platform

Ready for integration by mid-2029.

17 months connecting the platform to the arena's physical systems as they come online. Load and resilience testing against real conditions. Operational tuning that only makes sense against a working venue.

Deliverables

Full systems integration

Access control, POS, payment gateways, 3D visualisation.

Load-tested platform

Validated against real arena traffic.

Production rollout on 1 December 2030, with heightened support through the first high-traffic events. Three knowledge-transfer workshops leave your teams able to run and evolve the platform without depending on us.

Deliverables

Live platform

1 December 2030.

Knowledge transfer

Content operations, delivery process, technical handover.

Delivery schedule

53 months to go-live, five phases, firm at signature.

Fans see the platform on one date, 1 December 2030. You see accepted, working releases continuously from 2027. One public launch, not one big-bang risk. Hypercare and steady-state support follow separately.

The team

Investment, before combined-award discount

Both tenders, priced separately as the procurement requires.

Each tender stands on its own. Awarded together, the combined-award discount on the next slide brings this down by 1,29M€.

Website tender

3.905.000€

Mobile app tender

4.665.000€

8,57M€

The case for awarding both

One team across both tenders costs less than two teams across one each.

Both tenders8,57M€ Less 15%1,29M€ Combined price7,28M€

7,28M€

Combined price, both tenders

A 1,29M€ saving against the standalone totals, from one joint Discovery, one design foundation, and one accountable team across both surfaces.

Let's build it.
